QF.id: XXVA Sales and Distribution of Research Services_22

question.text: The concept of 'pre-deal research' in the context of research services refers to:

answer.text: Research conducted before a client makes any investment decision
Research that can be published during the cooling-off period around investment banking transactions, as an exception to the blackout rules
Research conducted by interns before they become full-time analysts
Research that is only shared with the research entity's internal team

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Pre-deal research is specifically referenced in the proviso to Regulation 18(4)-(6) as an exception — RAs can engage in investor education activities including publication of pre-deal research even during periods when solicitation-related activities are restricted. **The Trap:** Pre-deal research has a specific regulatory meaning — it's not general pre-investment research or intern work. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XV covers pre-deal research as an exception to the research-banking separation rules. Series IX covers it from the investment banking perspective. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Pre-deal research must still be objective and comply with all disclosure requirements — it's an exception to the blackout on analyst activities during banking deals, not an exception to research quality standards.
